name: Nintendo Entertainment System
description: The NES was Nintendo's iconic 8-bit home console and launched classic franchises like Mario and Zelda, popularizing console gaming worldwide.
funFact: In Japan, the NES was called the Famicom, and its controller included a built-in microphone.

codingExamples.title: Change Background Color (6502 Assembly)
explanation: LDA #$01 loads the value 1 into the accumulator register.
STA $2001 writes the value to the PPU mask register to change background rendering settings.

websites.title: NESDev Wiki
websites.description: Comprehensive technical documentation for NES development.

websites.title: Making NES Games
websites.description: Simple online 6502 assembler and tutorials.

name: Super Nintendo Entertainment System
description: Nintendo's 16-bit follow-up to the NES, with advanced graphics, Mode 7 effects, and legendary games like Super Mario World and Zelda: A Link to the Past.
funFact: The SNES's sound chip was designed by Sony, paving the way for future collaborations.

codingExamples.title: Set Background Color (65c816 Assembly)
explanation: LDA #$1F loads the accumulator with hexadecimal 1F, which sets the display brightness.
STA $2100 writes that value to the INIDISP register, updating the display color.

websites.title: SNES Dev Wiki
websites.description: Community SNES homebrew and development documentation.

websites.title: Geiger's SNES Documentation
websites.description: Detailed SNES docs and tools for assembly programming.

name: Nintendo 64
description: Nintendo's first 3D console, famous for iconic games like Super Mario 64 and Zelda: Ocarina of Time.
funFact: The controller's three-pronged design was intended for different game control styles.

codingExamples.title: Print Hello (C, libultra SDK)
explanation: Includes the libultra SDK for N64 development.
Defines mainproc, the N64 program entry point.
osSyncPrintf prints a string to the debug console.

websites.title: N64 Dev Wiki
websites.description: Documentation and tutorials for developing on the Nintendo 64.

websites.title: libdragon SDK
websites.description: Open-source library for N64 homebrew.

name: PlayStation
description: Sony's first console brought 3D gaming to the masses, with hits like Final Fantasy VII and Metal Gear Solid.
funFact: The PlayStation was nearly a Nintendo device before Sony went solo.

codingExamples.title: Draw Text (C, PSY-Q SDK)
explanation: Includes the PSY-Q SDK headers for graphics and font.
Initializes the font console at position (0,0).
Prints 'Hello, PlayStation!' on the screen.

websites.title: PSXDev
websites.description: Resources and tools for PlayStation 1 homebrew and development.

name: PlayStation 2
description: The best-selling console of all time, with a vast library and multimedia capabilities.
funFact: The PS2 could run Linux with the official Linux kit, becoming a low-cost hobbyist computer.

codingExamples.title: Print Text (C, PS2SDK)
explanation: Includes header for PS2 debug output.
Initializes the screen.
scr_printf prints 'Hello, PS2!' on the display.

websites.title: PS2DEV.org
websites.description: Community for PlayStation 2 homebrew development.

name: Xbox
description: Microsoft's first game console, known for Halo and robust homebrew with modchips.
funFact: The original Xbox used a modified PC motherboard and components.

codingExamples.title: Basic Hello (C, XDK/SDLx)
explanation: Includes Xbox debug header.
Prints a hello message to the debug output.
Loops forever to keep the system running.

websites.title: Xbox Dev Wiki
websites.description: Extensive Xbox hardware and software internals.

name: Nintendo GameCube
description: Nintendo's compact and powerful cube-shaped console, famous for Super Smash Bros. Melee.
funFact: GameCube controller ports are still featured on modern Nintendo consoles for Smash Bros. fans.

codingExamples.title: Print to Screen (C, libogc/devkitPPC)
explanation: Initializes the GameCube video hardware.
Initializes a simple text console.
Prints a hello message.
Infinite loop keeps the console open.

websites.title: DevKitPro
websites.description: Toolchains and docs for GameCube and Wii homebrew.

name: Sega Genesis
description: Sega's 16-bit console, famed for fast action, Sonic the Hedgehog, and rivalry with Nintendo.
funFact: Known as the Mega Drive outside North America.

codingExamples.title: Print Message (C, SGDK)
explanation: Includes the Sega Genesis Development Kit library.
Draws text at screen coordinates (10, 13).
Infinite loop keeps the message displayed.

websites.title: SGDK Docs
websites.description: Official SGDK repository, tools and docs for Genesis development.

name: Game Boy
description: Nintendo's portable 8-bit phenomenon, with Tetris introducing gaming to millions on the go.
funFact: Astronauts took a Game Boy to space on a mission in 1993.

codingExamples.title: Infinite Loop (Z80 Assembly, RGBDS)
explanation: Defines the start label.
Jumps back to the same label, creating a loop.

websites.title: GBDev Wiki
websites.description: The go-to resource for all aspects of Game Boy development.

name: Game Boy Advance
description: A 32-bit portable powerhouse, delivering SNES-like games on the go.
funFact: The GBA can play all classic Game Boy and Color titles.

codingExamples.title: Fill Screen With Color (C, devkitARM)
explanation: Sets display mode 3 (bitmap).
Fills the screen with blue color (0x1F in 15-bit color).
Infinite loop keeps the image visible.

websites.description: Legendary in-depth GBA programming tutorial.

name: Nintendo DS
description: Dual screens and a touchscreen made the DS a huge innovation in portable gaming.
funFact: The Nintendo DS Lite was the highest-selling model of the DS family.

Waits for vertical blank to save power.

name: PlayStation Portable
description: Sony's portable system with 3D graphics, media playback, and a huge homebrew community.
funFact: The PSP can emulate many earlier consoles thanks to its powerful hardware.

name: Cisco IOS Router
description: Cisco routers form the backbone of the internet, offering enterprise networking and secure data transport. They are managed via a command-line interface.
funFact: The 'write memory' command is still supported from the 80s.

codingExamples.title: Set Hostname
explanation: Enters configuration mode.
Sets the hostname to Router01.
Exits configuration mode.

websites.title: Cisco CLI Analyzer

name: Linksys WRT54G Router
description: Legendary home Wi-Fi router beloved for its hackable firmware and robustness.
funFact: A huge community built custom firmware for WRT54G, unlocking pro networking features.

explanation: Writes the word 'blink' to the router's power LED control to blink the power LED.

websites.title: DD-WRT Wiki
websites.description: Full guides for modding and programming Linksys WRT54G routers.

name: Raspberry Pi as Router
description: Use a Raspberry Pi as a configurable home router or firewall using Linux routing tools like dnsmasq and iptables.
funFact: A Pi can run as an enterprise-grade firewall using software like OpenWRT or pfSense.

explanation: Enables IPv4 packet forwarding, an essential setting for using the Pi as a router.

funFact: The C64's SID sound chip is still prized by musicians and chip-tune artists.

name: Raspberry Pi
description: A tiny affordable computer that lets anyone learn programming and electronics.
funFact: Over 40 million Raspberry Pis have been sold worldwide.

codingExamples.title: Say Hello (Python 3)
explanation: This prints the words 'Hello, world!' on the screen.
Try editing the message and see what changes!

codingExamples.title: Blink an LED (Python, GPIO Zero)
codingExamples.code:
from gpiozero import LED
from time import sleep

led = LED(17)

while True:
    led.on()
    sleep(1)
    led.off()
    sleep(1)
explanation: Imports the library to control the Pi's pins.
Sets up an LED on pin 17.
Turns the LED on and off every second, making it blink.

websites.title: Raspberry Pi Projects
websites.description: Official easy-to-follow coding and hardware tutorials.

websites.title: GPIO Zero Documentation
websites.description: Docs for controlling electronics easily on the Raspberry Pi.

name: ZX Spectrum
description: The ZX Spectrum launched the UK home programming boom with cheap computers and colorful games.
funFact: Games loaded from cassette tapes, accompanied by screeching sounds.

explanation: Prints a message to the screen.
GO TO 10 repeats forever, scrolling the message.

name: MAME Arcade
description: MAME is the Multiple Arcade Machine Emulator, running classic arcade games for preservation and study.

name: Neo Geo
description: The Neo Geo powered both arcades and 'luxury' home consoles with advanced 2D graphics and fighting games.
funFact: Neo Geo home cartridges were the size of a VHS tape!
